reviews.review_title: I was so excited but lacks music options
reviews.review_description: The concept was so fun and the playing it you could see the potential but, if you lay out the game board piano style and can’t play with your fingers with accuracy it’s a little disappointing. But the big thing was the lack of music what would go well with the style of rhythm game they have set up. Like the music they have doesn’t feel very rhythm game at all. I won’t play this again until maybe they update their library. ￼I would play this if they had just like two rhythm game songs but there isn’t any currently. Please get some music you can dance and get in the flow with.
20260411 APT AND OTHER EXTRA SONGS ARE DISAPPEAR AFTER LATEST UPDATE.

So much fun!!
Can’t wait until the song mapping app comes out.
This game has really good hand tracking but sometimes it barely works but overall this game is good and if you like rhythm games this game is a great pick
Its a fun game but needs alot more songs, more popular songs.

Needs more official songs. Would be willing to pay for licensed music as well. $1-2 per song or $6-8 for music packs seems fair. Custom songs are a nice bonus, but there aren't many popular songs in there.

I really enjoy music games, smash drums and ragnarock being my favorite. I decided to check this one out. Generally I hate hand tracking and it never works well but this is the exception to that. The hand tracking is spot on and very accurate. It's a lot of fun playing the songs. More major label music would make the game more enjoyable and there isn't a lot of content but it's only 10 bucks. For the price the content is fair and it really is a blast to play.

Love the hand tracking concept. Also the music tracks are nothing I’ve ever heard before. I do t know if I’m just old or if all these music artists are from different countries? But they are very catchy. Love the game. Oh and the response to perfect, good or miss is spot on. I’d love to see less Indy and maybe more mainstream or EDM tracks? That would be a blast!

The best part about this game are the hand mechanics — they work very well. The level design and song choices are questionable, but still fun. The only problem is that, my first time playing the game, I was able to beat the hardest songs on the hardest difficulty in one attempt. This game by itself is not much of a challenge, so I must rely on community levels for a challenging experience.
It’s so glitchy and it is crashing my headset

I think its a good game but its also not that good i think the idea of hand tracking is great but hand tracking on a game like that is also bad beacuse you need to use your hands alod and my quest cant handel that cuse its to fast so i think its good but to fast

Great game with good potential, congratulations to the developers! I want support for Bhaptics Tactsleeve, for more immersion!


Added later. I don't think the game will be a hit, as the hand tracking delay is still there, and timing is extremely important in this kind of games. Developers, make a version of the game with joysticks (let it be sticks hitting a xylophone), while the game is in early access, you can afford such an experiment and get on the hardcore path in time! Good luck!

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the 5-star review and thoughtful feedback — we’re really glad you see the potential in BEATABLE, and we appreciate your kind words! ❤️

Bhaptics support is a great suggestion — we love the idea of adding more immersive feedback layers and will definitely keep that in mind as we continue developing.

As for hand tracking, we know it can feel different depending on the environment and motion style. That said, when performed with solid technique, it can be extremely accurate — here’s a video of LSToast, one of the top rhythm game players, full comboing the hardest level in BEATABLE using hand tracking:
👉 https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=JxDV3_PoZLQ

That said, you're not alone in wanting more input options — we’re currently exploring controller support as a potential accessibility feature. Your xylophone-style idea is clever and definitely on our radar.

Thanks again for your support and constructive ideas — we’re in early access because we want this kind of feedback. We’ll keep pushing to make BEATABLE even better.

You’re welcome to join our Discord to hear the latest updates:
👉 https://discord.gg/TcNRzTaMp5

reviews.review_description: It's feels just right to tap on the table with the beat. This game Should receive more love and noise level on social media . Hope the developers release more sons soon

Very well made. For those that complained limited songs, 2 things first, the dev may sell packs like beat saber so don't dog on that, 24 songs is awesome with the price of the app. Secondly, custom tracks is being incorporated so the possibilites will be endless. I highly recommend and you can't beat the price just tap ✋tap ✋ tap ✋ tap ✋ that buy now button you'll enjoy.

Right out of the gate, I fell deeply deeply in love with this game. This has quickly risen to the top of my top 3 rhythm games, next to beat saber and synth riders. Very smooth and responsive game play and feels so intuitive. You can tell the developers put a lot of love and time into getting it right. And they got it right. If I’m loving this game this much at launch, I can’t wait to see where it goes. Oh, and you can do custom songs right out of the gate if you want to personalize it, though I haven’t messed with the custom songs yet. But just seeing that I could, I knew my 10 bucks would be quickly dispatched. Thank you XR Games. You’ve killed it with this one!

The whole concept is executed perfectly. It's a top tier use of Mixed Reality and playing on a hard surface is truly immersive. Only thing I'd love to see are Quest Achievements to hunt through like other rhythm games (i.e. Beat Saber, Audica, Just Dance VR, and many more)

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the fantastic 5-star review! ❤️

We’re thrilled you’re enjoying the concept and immersion — hearing that BEATABLE feels top-tier in MR really means a lot.

And yes — Quest Achievements are a great idea, and you’re not the only one asking! It’s already in our backlog and something we’d love to include in a future update. Stay tuned 👀

Feel free to join our Discord for the latest updates:
👉 https://discord.gg/TcNRzTaMp5

reviews.review_title: A new, unique experience for rhythm fans
reviews.review_description:
I've started playing the game today and it's *so good*!

I almost exclusively play rhythm games in VR, and even though I've already played so many of them, I'm amazed by how unique a new one can still feel! 

BEATABLE is for people like me, who love tapping to the beat on anything they can find 😆 

It's fascinating how precise the hand tracking is, even if you need to move very fast. 

And tapping on a physical object is so much more satisfying than just hitting the air like in most VR rhythm games.

It's absolutely worth the price if you enjoy rhythm games at all.

That's what I love about VR: There is still so much space to create an entirely new experience with a simple idea!

I had debated about whether to get this game, partly because I was unsure of the hand tracking capabilities, and partly because I had it in my head it wouldn't be as fun as a full body rhythm game.

Happy to say I was very wrong on both counts. The tracking is superb and it is a blast to play. Simultaneously simple but tricky, with finger clicks and hand claps making me feel like this is the musical equivalent of trying to pat your head and rub your belly at times, yet a great sense of satisfaction in rattling off a fast paced sequence of notes.

As simple as the overall design for the game may seem, it's nicely crafted, with some custom options for the table and virtual hands, which all looks very polished. And of course there's pass through which works great with this style of game.

My only issue is very minor, in that I look forward to seeing the library expanded due to the limited selection of tracks that I would prefer to play. Not a big deal, it'll hopefully happen naturally over time.

Aside from that, I have a tendency to keep reaching towards the notes on the track instead of keeping my hands on the designated area which is a me problem, but I think it also shows how the game sucks you in.

I think any fan of rhythm games will enjoy this one, and it's pretty accessible to those who are less game oriented as it does away with controllers, buttons, and the worry of throwing yourself into the nearest appliance. So I'd recommend giving it a shot if you're still on the fence.

Game is a blast to play and work well. I really feel connected to the concept of this game and the devs did a great job. As always with any music rhythm game the updated track list and fresh content keeps us coming back. So really leaving a review here to rally support for continued content so this great concept can go the distance! Pick it up, worth it and fun!

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the amazing 5-star review! ❤️

We’re thrilled to hear how much you’re enjoying BEATABLE, and especially that the concept really connected with you — that means a lot to the team. You’re absolutely right: fresh content is what keeps rhythm games alive and thriving.

The good news is we’ve got more free songs on the way, plus our custom song & map editor is launching very soon, so the community will be able to create and share new content to keep things endlessly fresh.

Thanks again for the incredible support — reviews like yours truly help this concept go the distance!

reviews.review_title: Great game, but needs more songs
reviews.review_description:
It is a great rhythm game that responds well to the hand tracking, and the tactile sensation from hitting a table and getting visual feedback is great. 

However, the game needs at least 12 more songs for it to be even close to a complete package. 

I’m also hoping the store will open soon so at least we can purchase song packs.

It’s been a month since release so I find it strange the store isn’t open yet. I hope the devs are still at work on more content because this game is fun and unique in the rhythm games genre.

Update:

Thanks to the dev for the great news regarding custom tracks, and more content. This Will definitely increase my score to a five star rating once it’s implemented.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the thoughtful 4-star review — and for recognising what makes BEATABLE feel fun and unique! ❤️

We’re really glad the hand tracking and tactile feedback are working well for you — that table-slap rhythm is a core part of the experience. And you’re absolutely right: more songs are key. The custom song and map editor launches next month, so players will soon be able to create and share their own tracks.

As for the store, we’ve been heads-down focused on building new features and adding songs, but we do plan to share a full content roadmap soon — including store-based expansions.

Thanks again for your feedback and for being part of the early BEATABLE community — we’re just getting started!

P.S. — As you’re enjoying BEATABLE, we’d love it if you mentioned it to your friends. We’ve got next to zero marketing budget, so word of mouth really helps! 😊

reviews.review_description: The music are good. Cant wait to have more. The hand detection is quite good. For the price its a banger.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the 5-star review! ❤️

We’re so glad you’re enjoying the music and the hand tracking — and love that you feel it’s a banger for the price!

More free songs are coming, and if you’re curious about community-made content, the custom song & map editor is already live.

👉 Join our Discord to see how to access them:
https://discord.gg/TcNRzTaMp5

reviews.review_description: I love rhythm games and this is amazing this is the only thing I play now you guys did amazing and I would love if you add a ton of updates only problem is there's not that many songs but I know there going to add more and maybe add custom songs so there are a ton of songs 10/10 game the gameplay is so good too like I thought the hand tracking would like bug out but it never did so props to you guys 10/10 game

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the incredible 5-star review — we’re so happy to hear BEATABLE has become your new favourite game! ❤️

It means the world to hear you're loving the gameplay and that the hand tracking is holding up smoothly — we’ve worked hard to make it feel reliable and fun. And yes, we hear you on the song count! We’re actively working on adding more free songs right now. Plus, we’re building a custom song/map editor so the community can create and share even more content.

Thanks again for the love — we’re just getting started, and your support keeps us going! 🎶

P.S. — As you’re enjoying BEATABLE, we’d love it if you mentioned it to your friends. We’ve got next to zero marketing budget, so we’re relying on word of mouth to help spread the word! 😊

reviews.review_description:
So, 

The concept and execution is an absolute 5/5!!

Immediate Dev response FAR outweighed any negative star ratings…. If you wanna REALLY feel the beat  get this game. If you don’t wanna feel the beat, buy it for someone else who does….. then buy it for yourself after anyhow  

In all seriousness, the mechanics in this gem are u like anything ever before in VR… ever  

Peace!

developer_response.body:
Appreciate the honest feedback — and fair play for calling it like you see it. Glad you rated the core concept and gameplay 5/5, that means a lot.

On the music front: totally hear you. That said, we’re not just pulling royalty-free tracks off SoundCloud — BEATABLE already features music from 3× platinum-selling artist Tom Zanetti, and even a track from RDB, who’ve collaborated with Snoop Dogg. So while it might not all be instantly recognisable yet, there’s real quality and pedigree in there.

That said, you're right — we absolutely need to keep pushing for bigger and more iconic tunes. Got a top 10 wish list? Send it my way: bobby.thandi@xrgames.io. I’ll do everything I can to chase them down.

Hope to see you back soon. Peace!

P.S. — as you’re enjoying BEATABLE, we’d love it if you mentioned it to your friends. We’ve got next to zero marketing budget, so we’re relying on word of mouth to help spread the word!

reviews.review_description: Licensed music please

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the 4-star review! ❤️

Totally hear you on the licensed music — we’re working hard on that right now, and it’s definitely on the way. Stay tuned for updates soon!

You’re welcome to join our Discord to hear the latest:
👉 https://discord.gg/TcNRzTaMp5

reviews.review_title: best rhythm game, both new and familiar
reviews.review_description:
The game lowers the entry barrier with a familiar keyboard rhythm style, but the gameplay has been innovatively adapted for VR. The fact that all controls and gameplay are possible solely through hand tracking offers an experience that traditional console rhythm games cannot replicate. When you start the game, it feels as if you are standing in front of a rhythm game console booth in a vast arcade.
I hope that in the future, you will allow us to change the game's background skin as well.

The UI is intuitive, clean, and streamlined by removing unnecessary elements. The gameplay also breaks away from simply tapping keys by incorporating hand gesture movements, which adds variety and prevents monotony. It would be exciting to see elements like a DJ turntable added in the future.

However, it is disappointing that the number of playable songs is still quite limited. A custom mode, which is almost a standard feature in modern rhythm games, is urgently needed. Additionally, the game currently only supports English, which may be a drawback for players who are not comfortable with the language. Expanding the supported languages would be beneficial.

Since the game is still in early access and the developers are actively communicating with users, showing a passion for game development, my feelings lean more towards anticipation rather than concern. I look forward to the future development of this game!

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the incredible 5-star review — this kind of detailed feedback really means a lot to us!

We’re thrilled you’re enjoying BEATABLE and the way it blends the familiarity of classic rhythm games with a fresh, VR-first experience. Creating that feeling of stepping into a rhythm arcade booth was exactly what we were aiming for, so it’s amazing to hear that resonated with you.

We’ve taken note of all your suggestions — background skin options, custom song mode, and even ideas like a DJ turntable — all very cool directions we’d love to explore as the game grows.
And you’re absolutely right: expanding the language options is on our radar, and we agree that it’s important for making the game accessible to more players worldwide.

As for songs — we’re currently working on 12 new tracks, which will be added for free in future updates. And we’re actively exploring ways to expand that even further through licensed music and community-driven features.

Thanks again for your support and belief in what we’re building. We can’t wait to show you what’s coming next!

P.S. — If you’re enjoying BEATABLE, we’d love it if you mentioned it to your friends. We’ve got next to zero marketing budget, so we’re relying on word of mouth to help spread the word! 😊

reviews.review_description:
Been excited for this release for a couple weeks now, and it certainly didn't disappoint after my first bunch of song playthroughs.
Really, really enjoyable and implemented beautifully. Massive kudos to the devs.
I wonder if going out today to purchase a yoga mat to drape over my table will be a big brain move.

developer_response.body:
Love this — thanks so much for the 5 stars and the kind words! Amazing to hear BEATABLE lived up to the hype for you. Huge shoutout to you for the yoga mat idea too — honestly, that might just be the ultimate big brain move. Appreciate the support — loads more on the way!

P.S. — as you’re enjoying BEATABLE, we’d love it if you mentioned it to your friends. We’ve got next to zero marketing budget, so we’re relying on word of mouth to help spread the word!

reviews.review_title: As a Stepmania fan, thank you
reviews.review_description:
This is exactly the type of game I’ve been looking for VR. I used to be a huge fan of Stepmania, In The Groove, FlashFlashRevolution and DDR, and this scratches an itch to play those types of games in VR. I’m also a casual finger/table drummer so glad to have that scored now.

Feature request: I don’t know how to snap my fingers and dislike the feeling when I try to do it. A setting that converts all snaps into claps might be desirable? However, if the snapping movement is forgiving, this is not a must.

Otherwise, regularly adding songs/tracks would be great, and I have no doubt that this will happen. While I don’t know how this would work licensing-wise, the ability to add own tracks and convert Stepmania beat maps into BEATABLE versions would be amazing.

Thank you for this game and I am excited to see how it develops!

developer_response.body:
We’re thrilled BEATABLE scratches that rhythm game itch in VR — especially for fellow table tappers and rhythm purists.

Totally hear you on finger snapping — the good news is, you don’t need to produce a perfect finger snap for the system to detect it. As long as you make an effort, it will register just fine. This short video of our Creative Director playing one of the hardest levels might help show how forgiving it is:
👉 https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=adWSvKvoC-A

That said, we’re actively exploring accessibility options — though we’ll need to balance that carefully with scoring integrity. But we hear you.

As for content: we’ve just added 5 new tracks, more free music is coming, and we're building an editor to support custom songs /maps. We’re also working hard to try and secure music label deals for more well-known tracks down the line.

Thanks again for your support and thoughtful feedback — this is exactly the kind of insight that helps shape the game’s future!

P.S. — As you’re enjoying BEATABLE, we’d love it if you mentioned it to your friends. We’ve got next to zero marketing budget, so we’re relying on word of mouth to help spread the word! 😊

reviews.review_description: This game is cool and fun to play took a few games to get it into sync with it but when it does you feel the magic fill your soul!! Then you get to level 3 on the song difficulty and suddenly it's starts too give you that feeling of trying to rub your head whilst tapping your belly at times as your tapping sliding holding clapping and finger snapping all  in time to the music that's where the real skill to this game comes in hope there is plenty more to come for this game two suggestions one can we have an option for palm up gesture come here fingers you get the jyst as some of us can barely fingersnap two pad input as I can see people dancing in there front rooms as I found you can air drum which is a great way to play! BIG MASSIVE WELL DONE DEVS😁😁keep them tunes coming dnb anyone bwahahahaha!!!!

developer_response.body:
Oh boi — what a review!! 😁 Thanks so much for the 5 stars and the love! That moment when the rhythm syncs and it clicks — we live for that. And yes, Level 3 is where BEATABLE really starts flexing your skills… love the “rub your head while tapping your belly” comparison — spot on!

We’re buzzing that you’re thinking about gestures and play styles too — palm-up and pad input ideas are great shouts. We’re always listening, so feedback like this is gold. And yep, more tunes are coming — 12 new tracks already in the works and rolling out soon. DnB? Let’s just say… you’re not the only one asking 👀

Massive thanks again — you made our day! 💥🥁🔥

P.S. — as you’re enjoying BEATABLE, we’d love it if you mentioned it to your friends. We’ve got next to zero marketing budget, so we’re relying on word of mouth to help spread the word!

reviews.review_description:
It's a good start.
I like the idea and it's well implemented.

Aside from the lack of musics, here are a few minor feedbacks :
*We have to validate several pages before launching a music (in other similar games, we can launch a music directly from the music selection menu).
*The difficulty always resets to the easiest one instead of keeping our last tried one in memory or setting a global difficulty.
*We need to click a music to see our score (I would prefer to see them in the music selection menu).
*In the tutorial, the page about audio/input latency doesn't have an image or video. Since it didn't attract my attention and was the last screen, I skipped it (which was an error on my part since it's quite important).

Lastly, I think this game would work very well with music with a clear partition for percussions (drum, cymbal, clave ...).

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much for the 5-star review and thoughtful feedback!

We're really glad you're enjoying the game and appreciate you taking the time to share some constructive ideas. We'll be doing a deep dive on feedback now the game is launched and these ideas will be shared for sure!

Re tutorial--we'll going to add a Tutorial that you play through and make it more prominent for first time users for sure.

And yes, we completely agree: the game shines with percussion-heavy tracks. We’re working on adding more songs, including some that really lean into that rhythm and beat-driven vibe.

Appreciate your support—it really helps us shape what's next for BEATABLE!

P.S. — If you’re enjoying BEATABLE, we’d love it if you mentioned it to your friends. We’ve got next to zero marketing budget, so we’re relying on word of mouth to help spread the word!

reviews.review_description:
My Quest 3's left controller stopped working, since then, I've been searching for apps and games that can be played with handtracking, and have found a few. Maestro, Job and Vacation Simulator, etc. but none of them have felt as responsive and fun like this. Beatable is the perfect handtracking music game, it scratches almost every itch.

The final thing that I'm hopeful will be included is custom songs, that would be the cherry on the cake.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the amazing 5-star review! ❤️

We’re thrilled that BEATABLE has been such a great hand-tracking experience for you — especially after your left controller stopped working. Creating a game that feels responsive, intuitive, and fun with just your hands was exactly what we set out to do!

And you’re in luck: custom songs are on the roadmap! We’re currently working on an editor that will allow players to add their own custom songs and beat maps.
In the meantime, we’ve also just added 5 new tracks (including 2 EDM songs) — and more free songs are on the way.

Thanks again for your kind words and support — it really motivates us to keep evolving BEATABLE!

P.S. — As you’re enjoying BEATABLE, we’d love it if you mentioned it to your friends. We’ve got next to zero marketing budget, so we’re relying on word of mouth to help spread the word! 😊

reviews.review_description:
Went in expecting to refund the game and stayed for about an hour playing with an ear to ear grin on my face. This game is extremely satisfying. No need for controllers for haptics using your hands is all the haptics you need. These Songs are absolutely the PERFECT songs to play to.

It basically feels like when you’re bored and you’re tapping the table but instead of music in your head you hear real music and actually have guides on how to tap to the beat.

If anyone’s ever played the mobile sensation “Piano Tiles” it’s practically the same thing but your hands instead of your thumbs. 

I’m wayyy underestimating how good this game is I’m not phrasing it well but the point is is that you need to but this game while it’s cheap. Please I beg you the more money these people get the more music we get.

developer_response.body:
WOW — thank you for this incredible 5-star review! 💛

We’re so glad BEATABLE surprised you in the best possible way. That image of you grinning from ear to ear while tapping to the beat is exactly what we hoped for — simple, intuitive, and satisfying.

We love the comparison to Piano Tiles — but with your whole hands instead of just thumbs — that’s such a fun way to put it.
And yes — using your own hands as the haptics? That’s part of the magic ✨

We’ve just dropped 2 new EDM tracks, and we’ve got 10 more free songs on the way — plus big plans to keep expanding the music lineup with your support.

Thanks again for backing the game and spreading the love — it truly helps us bring more music to life in BEATABLE!

P.S. — As you’re enjoying BEATABLE, we’d love it if you mentioned it to your friends. We’ve got next to zero marketing budget, so we’re relying on word of mouth to help spread the word! 😊

reviews.review_description: Bought this on a whim, the mechanics and calibration are amazing and intuitive, never seen a hand tracking game work so well! The instant VR/MR switch is a really cool touch, this is a great experience if it seems like you might like it but you're unsure, just get it. Well worth the price

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the awesome 5-star review — we’re so glad you took a chance on BEATABLE! ❤️

It’s fantastic to hear the mechanics and calibration felt intuitive — we spent a lot of time making sure hand tracking feels natural and responsive. And yes, we love that instant VR/MR switch too — it’s a small touch, but it really adds to the immersion!

Thanks again for the kind words — and for encouraging others to give it a go. Your support means the world to the team!

P.S. — As you’re enjoying BEATABLE, we’d love it if you mentioned it to your friends. We’ve got next to zero marketing budget, so we’re relying on word of mouth to help spread the word! 😊

reviews.review_description:
Beatable is a must-have for people who are into rhythm/music based games. The hand tracking aspect and feedback when slapping (not too hard) your table is genius!

I played through all the tracks (18, I believe, since the date of this review) in one sitting. I'm glad the devs will be adding more songs, along with the ability for custom songs down the road.

I'm looking forward to the new content!

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the fantastic 5-star review! ❤️

We’re thrilled that you’re enjoying BEATABLE and that the hand tracking + table tapping feedback felt so good — that tactile connection is exactly what we aimed for.

It’s amazing you played through all 18 tracks in one sitting! We’re excited to share that more free songs are already in the pipeline, and as you mentioned, we’re actively working on an editor so players can add their own custom songs and beat maps in the future.

Thanks again for your support and excitement — we can’t wait to show you even more content coming to BEATABLE soon!

P.S. — As you’re enjoying BEATABLE, we’d love it if you mentioned it to your friends. We’ve got next to zero marketing budget, so we’re relying on word of mouth to help spread the word! 😊

reviews.review_description: This is an amazing game! The hand tracking is incredibly precise, and tapping on the table provides a great sense of feedback. However, the music selection feels a bit limited—I hope future updates will support player mods or custom music. I'd be willing to pay double for that feature!

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the 5-star review — we’re so glad you’re loving BEATABLE! 💛

It’s great to hear the hand tracking is feeling precise and that tapping on your own surface is delivering that satisfying feedback — that’s exactly the kind of immersive experience we aimed for.

You’re right about the current music selection being limited — we’ve just added 2 new EDM tracks, and 10 more free tracks are on the way.

Even better, we’re working on an editor that will allow players to add custom music and beat maps — we know how much the community wants this, and it’s on the roadmap.

Thank you again for the support — it truly means a lot!

P.S. — As you’re enjoying BEATABLE, we’d love it if you mentioned it to your friends. We’ve got next to zero marketing budget, so we’re relying on word of mouth to help spread the word! 😊

reviews.review_description:
This game is sooo good..hand tracking is amazing as well I'm using my bed as the slapping  pad no problem 😁
Plus love the fact you can use earbuds and the game  adjusts the latency for you in the options..
Game changer for me ...
Thanks guys keep up the great work ..over 50 here and  I'm still rocking the rhythm games ..
Game feels very natural to play

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the 5-star review — we love hearing this! 💛

We’re thrilled that BEATABLE is clicking with you — and huge props for using your bed as a slapping pad, that’s the kind of creativity we love to see 😄 I find the audio feedback and difference in tactile feedback in using your bed gives a different feel to the game!

It’s also great to hear that the hand tracking and latency sync with earbuds are working smoothly — we wanted the game to feel natural and accessible for everyone, and your feedback really means a lot.

Massive respect for still rocking rhythm games — you’re proof that rhythm has no age limit 🎶

Thanks again for the kind words and support!

P.S. — As you’re enjoying BEATABLE, we’d love it if you mentioned it to your friends. We’ve got next to zero marketing budget, so we’re relying on word of mouth to help spread the word! 😊

reviews.review_description: Already great, and custom songs incoming!

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the brilliant 5-star review! ❤️

We’re thrilled you’re enjoying BEATABLE — and yes, custom songs are coming this month, so get ready to create and play even more.

Thanks again for the support — we’re just getting started! 🎶

P.S. — If you’re enjoying BEATABLE, we’d love it if you shared it with your friends. We’ve got next to zero marketing budget, so word of mouth really helps! 😊

reviews.review_description: If your a rhythm gamer you will love this. Excellent use of MR and using the table as haptics for the hand tracking just feels right. Awesome game, hopefully it gets more songs in the future or a modding community that can contribute. Super cool!

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the amazing 5-star review! ❤️

We’re thrilled that BEATABLE resonated with you — especially as a rhythm gamer. The MR + tabletop combo was all about creating that intuitive, physical connection, so it’s great to hear it “just feels right” in your hands.

We’ve just added 5 new songs, with more free tracks on the way, and we’re actively working on an editor so players can add their own custom songs and charts. We’d love to see a strong modding community grow around the game too — so stay tuned!

Thanks again for the support — and keep on tapping! 🎶

P.S. — As you’re enjoying BEATABLE, we’d love it if you mentioned it to your friends. We’ve got next to zero marketing budget, so we’re relying on word of mouth to help spread the word! 😊

reviews.review_description: Very fun and well thought out game!  Anywhere is a drum! Maybe one day this will allow custom songs.

developer_response.body:
Hi,

This happens when your headset can still "see" your controllers. The simplest way to solve for this is to remove the batteries from the controllers first and then start the game BEATABLE. Pls do let me know how you get on at bobby.thandi@xrgames.io ?

Thanks

reviews.review_description: Good idea with near-perfect execution! Eagerly waiting for the ability to add custom songs..

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the 5-star review! ❤️

We’re thrilled you enjoyed the concept and felt the execution was nearly spot-on — that means a lot to the team!

And yes — we hear you loud and clear on custom content. Custom song and map support is coming next month, so you’ll soon be able to create and share your own tracks with the community. We can’t wait to see what you come up with!

Thanks again for the support — and stay tuned!

P.S. — As you’re enjoying BEATABLE, we’d love it if you mentioned it to your friends. We’ve got next to zero marketing budget, so word of mouth really helps! 😊

reviews.review_description:
I've had a fair share of rhythm games, Stepmania with keyboard, Osu! witb mouse and keyboard, Taiko No Tatsujin, Beat Saber and more. This one definitely takes the concept of Stepmania but with hand controls, which is something completely new to me.

The songs are great and the library is a bit on the short side, perhaps some more songs could provide us more gameplay, and modifiers would also improve the customization abilities.

The controls work flawlessly, with proper playing lf course, and i was getting a bit immersed that my neighbour was like "what kind of beat are you making? It sounds familiar", what a funny moment.

Overall, very good game, i really enjoyed it.

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much for the 5 stars and for sharing your rhythm game journey — love that you’ve played everything from Stepmania to Taiko to Osu! It’s awesome to hear that BEATABLE brought you something fresh with hand controls, and even better that it sparked a laugh with your neighbour! 😂

We totally hear you on the need for more content — 12 new tracks are already in the works and rolling out soon. Modifiers and extra layers of customization are also on our radar.

And that table-tap recognition moment? We know the feeling — in the studio, we’ve played so much that we can literally tell which song someone’s playing just by the rhythm of their desk taps. It’s a real thing!

Thanks again — thrilled you’re enjoying it! 🎶👏

P.S. — as you’re enjoying BEATABLE, we’d love it if you mentioned it to your friends. We’ve got next to zero marketing budget, so we’re relying on word of mouth to help spread the word!

reviews.review_description:
I wish i could put an 4.5 star instead of 4
To be honest this is simple in all way.
-Easy to setup
-Can be played almost everywhere
-Detection work wonderfully (Snap, Clap, tap)

My only complains not enought sound but i know it's an early access  ;)

Find some Music collabortation take a deal and you going to have must have VR mixed reality game.
A Way to allow custom song could be also an top notch option for community on the game

Hope you found a public and community for this game i start to be addict ;)

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the thoughtful review — 4.5 stars in spirit is more than good enough for us! 😄

We’re really glad you found BEATABLE simple, accessible, and fun to play anywhere. Hearing that snap/clap/tap detection feels great is a huge win for the team.

You’re absolutely right about the music. We’re already working on 12 new tracks that will be added for free in future updates, and we’re also having exciting conversations around licensed music partnerships.

Custom song support is something we’re actively exploring too—it would be amazing for building a community around the game.

Thanks again for the encouragement — it means the world!

P.S. — If you’re enjoying BEATABLE, we’d love it if you mentioned it to your friends. We’ve got next to zero marketing budget, so we’re relying on word of mouth to help spread the word! 😊

reviews.review_title: Incredible game with some small flaws
reviews.review_description:
I picked up this game a few days ago and am already loving it. It's the perfect companion to Beat Saber with a similar kind of tapping/cutting idea while finding a way to stay very original.
My only criticisms would be that the finger snapping recognition is a bit on and off, and that from my own tastes, the song selection is ok/mediocre past a few bangers.
But don't get me wrong, this game is insanely polished and absolutely brilliant, especially for the price and the fact that it's been out for only 2 weeks and in early access.
I really hope this game gets more attention, great job devs!

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the incredible 5-star review — we really appreciate the kind words and thoughtful feedback! ❤️

We’re thrilled that BEATABLE is clicking with you and complements your Beat Saber sessions — finding that balance between familiar and original was exactly our goal.

Finger snapping is a core part of the experience, and while it works reliably in most setups, there can occasionally be variation depending on lighting or hand position. If you're curious, check out this run from LSToast full comboing the hardest difficulty — maybe it’ll give you a few technique tips:
👉 https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=JxDV3_PoZLQ

Music taste is always personal, and we totally respect that. That said, we’ve just added 5 new tracks, and more free songs are already on the way. We're also working hard to secure deals with music labels, and an editor for custom songs and maps is in development — so the variety is only going to grow from here!

By the way — if you get a chance, I’d love to hear which songs you considered “bangers.” Feel free to drop me a line at bobby.thandi@xrgames.io — I’m always listening! 🎧

Thanks again for your support and positivity — it means a lot to the team!

P.S. — As you’re enjoying BEATABLE, we’d love it if you mentioned it to your friends. We’ve got next to zero marketing budget, so we’re relying on word of mouth to help spread the word! 😊

reviews.review_description:
I really enjoy VR for when it offers something new that's only possible using it, Beat Saber being one of my favorite examples, and while Beatable doesn't have as much content right now it's core gameplay is really fun and for $10, less than one music pack 
for Beat Saber, if you enjoy rhythm games it's absolutely worth picking up! 

I'm very impressed with how well it worked just using hand tracking. I also really appreciate the passthrough mode as I feel that's an underutilized feature. I did need to recalibrate the table every few songs, but the gesture to do that is easy and quick so no big issue. 

My biggest request right now is for more songs to be added as you can play through the entire catalog really quickly. 

Overall can't wait to see where it goes!

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the amazing review!

We completely agree—VR shines brightest when it offers something that just can’t be done any other way, and we’re honoured that you see BEATABLE in that space alongside games like Beat Saber.

We’re especially glad you enjoyed the hand tracking and passthrough mode—those were key to our vision for something fresh and intuitive. Re - recalibration - not noticed that myself. But will look into it! Thanks for mentioning it. 

Totally hear you on the song count—we’re already working on 12 new tracks that will be added for free in upcoming updates, and there’s more to come after that.

Thanks again for the kind words and support—it really means a lot!

P.S. — If you’re enjoying BEATABLE, we’d love it if you mentioned it to your friends. We’ve got next to zero marketing budget, so we’re relying on word of mouth to help spread the word!

reviews.review_description: Is there more i need to say? This is one fun game. Just like bear saber its very challenging and fun and I’m all in for that. Can’t wait for more songs

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the 5-star review — short and sweet, and we love it! ❤️

We’re so glad BEATABLE hit the mark for you — challenging and fun is exactly what we were aiming for. And yep, more songs are already on the way! We’ve just added 3x new tracks, and we’re working on more free music, plus a custom song editor so you can eventually play your own tracks too.

Thanks again for the support — and keep on tapping!

P.S. — As you’re enjoying BEATABLE, we’d love it if you mentioned it to your friends. We’ve got next to zero marketing budget, so we’re relying on word of mouth to help spread the word! 😊

reviews.review_description:
A cross between old school rhythm games (Guitar Hero, Tap sonic, DJ Max) and Donkey Konga!

As a die hard flat rhythm game fan (as someone that's spend probably £2k+ on music game controllers over the year) 
I can say Beatable mechanically is a very solid game.
The game takes full advantage of hand tracking to bring us an experience that's perfect for a Q3.

The team have some very good people at making charts. Some really nicely thought out patterns that play to each songs strengths.
With the added finger clicks and clapping......it almost reminds me of when I was a kid pretending to be drumming gods banging on pots and pans with friends 

Whilst I haven't had the opportunity to find a NOSTALGIA or a Chunithm arcade machine locally......Beatable is a very fine substitute to those title.

Whilst modest in song count....the game is also very modest in price.

Im hoping the game will continue to grow and expand after EA as it has potential to be easily in my top 5 VR rhythm games.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the incredible review!

As fellow rhythm game fans, your comparisons to Guitar Hero, DJ Max, and Donkey Konga made our day—and your comment about banging on pots and pans as a kid really captures the spirit we were going for!

We’re thrilled to hear you enjoyed the hand tracking and chart design. Our team put a lot of love into crafting patterns that feel satisfying and expressive, so it means a lot to hear that coming from someone who’s clearly got deep roots in the genre.

Totally agree on the song count—we're already working on expanding the soundtrack and have plans to keep evolving the game well beyond Early Access.

Thanks again for your support. Hope we can earn that spot in your top 5 for good!

P.S. — If you’re enjoying BEATABLE, we’d love it if you mentioned it to your friends. We’ve got next to zero marketing budget, so we’re relying on word of mouth to help spread the word!

reviews.review_description:
I REALLY LOVED THE CONCEPT!
So genius! Claping hands and the detection is SO GOOD! I am addicted to it! I hope that someday we get more songs with copyright to play! Even on Steam with DLCs with new songs!

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the incredible 5-star review! ❤️

We’re so glad the concept clicked with you — and that you’re enjoying the clap detection and overall feel of BEATABLE! Hearing that you're addicted (in the best way!) totally makes our day.

We’ve just added 5 new tracks, with more free songs on the way, and we’re actively working on securing deals with music labels to bring more licensed music into the game. We’re also exploring other platforms — including Steam — and would love to bring DLC song packs in the future!

Thanks again for the support and the energy — it really means a lot to the team! 🎶

P.S. — As you’re enjoying BEATABLE, we’d love it if you mentioned it to your friends. We’ve got next to zero marketing budget, so we’re relying on word of mouth to help spread the word! 😊

reviews.review_description: This game has great potential, and the dopamine kick you get by tapping fast is amazing. However, there aren't enough songs, which is a common issue in all of these rhythm vr games unless we can get custom songs. Sometimes, the snapping with your fingers feels a bit off, but most of the times it's fine. I also think this game picked the wrong name because when i Google it, all I get is beat saber

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the 4-star review — we really appreciate you taking the time to share your thoughts! ❤️

We’re thrilled that you’re enjoying the gameplay and that the dopamine hit from tapping is kicking in — that’s exactly the feeling we wanted to create!

You're right that more songs are needed — we’ve just added 5 new tracks (including 2 EDM songs and 3 more this week), and more free tracks are already on the way. We’re also excited to confirm that we’re working on an editor so players can add custom music and beat maps in the future.

Regarding finger snapping — thanks for mentioning it. Maybe this video can give you some tips: LSToast, one of the world’s best rhythm players, full comboed the hardest level in BEATABLE:
👉 https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=JxDV3_PoZLQ

And about the name — we hear you! Naming rhythm games in a crowded space isn’t easy, but we’re focused on making BEATABLE stand out through gameplay, updates, and community support.

Thanks again for playing and for helping us continue to improve BEATABLE!

P.S. — As you’re enjoying BEATABLE, we’d love it if you mentioned it to your friends. We’ve got next to zero marketing budget, so we’re relying on word of mouth to help spread the word! 😊

reviews.review_title: Devs make the game. Literally, right?
reviews.review_description:
That reply by the developer below is why I
 purchased my copy and gifted one as well to share in the "just one more time" that makes these games so addictive and satisfying because you can and will get good when that groove sets in.. what can I say? It's groovy 😎

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the amazing 5-star review — and for not just picking up BEATABLE but also gifting a copy too! That means the world to us 💛

It’s awesome to hear the groove has kicked in — that “just one more time” feeling is exactly what we hoped to capture. And yes, the dev replies come straight from the heart — we’re building this with the community, one beat at a time.

Thanks again for the support — stay groovy 😎

reviews.review_description:
It's a good game but not perfect, sometimes hits don't register for me, especially in faster parts and there isn't many songs. I'm guessing more will be added through dlc, I just hope they're not too expensive. 

Ps.. That Tom Zanetti song is just awful, actually painful to listen to and that Indian one about being a king. I was cringing through those two and I don't think I'll be able to bring myself to play those again so that's 2 less songs for me

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the 4-star review — we really appreciate the feedback 💛

If you're finding fast sections tricky, you might find this helpful: LSToast, one of the top rhythm game players in the world, full combo’ed the hardest level in BEATABLE (didn’t miss a single note). You can check out his run here — maybe his technique will help:
👉 https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=JxDV3_PoZLQ

As for the music — we completely understand that music is a very personal thing, and we respect your views. Just for context: Tom Zanetti is a triple platinum artist with hundreds of millions of streams, and Didn’t Know has over 63 million plays on Spotify. The Indian track is by RDB, whose song featured in one of the most iconic Bollywood films of all time.

That said — we hear you! We’re excited to share that 3 new songs are going live very soon, on top of 2 that recently dropped — so that’s 5 new tracks for you to check out very soon. All of which will be free. We'll share a Developer Update on the Store Page when it goes live.

Thanks again for playing BEATABLE — and for taking the time to share your thoughts. It really helps us grow the game.

P.S. — As you’re enjoying BEATABLE, we’d love it if you mentioned it to your friends. We’ve got next to zero marketing budget, so we’re relying on word of mouth to help spread the word! 😊

reviews.review_description: BEATABLE makes a strong entrance into the crowded rhythm game market by incorporating a fresh twist: it allows players to use their own physical surfaces as part of the gameplay. By tapping on chosen surfaces, clapping hands, and snapping fingers to hit the notes, the game offers a highly tactile experience that takes advantage of the headset’s hand-tracking features. However, this innovative approach does come with some challenges. The hand-tracking can struggle to keep up during fast movements at higher difficulty levels, leading to missed notes that can be detrimental in these settings - although this is inconsistent. If XR Games can resolve these hand-tracking issues and add popular licensed tracks through DLC soon, BEATABLE could become a must-have for VR gamers. Currently, it provides a fun, albeit brief, gaming experience, but without licensed music, it risks being overshadowed by more established titles in the genre. This would be unfortunate, as the game clearly has significant potential.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the thoughtful 4-star review — this kind of detailed feedback is incredibly valuable to us 💛

We’re glad you enjoyed the tactile twist in BEATABLE, and how the use of real-world surfaces, claps, and snaps adds to the physicality and fun of the experience. That’s exactly what we hoped to deliver with hand-tracking front and center.

You're right that high-speed sections can challenge the tracking system — that said, we find it's easier to use two hands on the same lane (one in front of the other) for the really fast notes. To see what's possible, check out LSToast (one of the world’s best Beat Saber and BEATABLE players) full combo the hardest level in the game using hand tracking:
👉 https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=JxDV3_PoZLQ

That said, I’d love to investigate the issue further — if you’re able to, please share a video of your own gameplay to me: bobby.thandi@xrgames.io and I'll look into where the hand tracking struggles to keep up for you. It would really help us pinpoint improvements.

As for music: 2 new EDM tracks just dropped, and 10 more free tracks are on the way — with plans to expand into licensed music as we grow. Custom music and beat maps will also be supported via an editor in the future, so players can create and share their own tracks too.

Thanks again for playing and for believing in the potential of BEATABLE. We're committed to pushing it forward!

P.S. — thanks for writing a review about BEATABLE on your website https://thevrrealm.com/reviews/beatable/. Loving seeing folks spread the word about BEATABLE!

reviews.review_title: The next generation of my gaming
reviews.review_description: This is my top game  since my dad bought this today you have to clap tap and snap which I am not very good at snapping for this game is getting a five star since I love this.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the 5-star review — and big shoutout to your dad for picking up BEATABLE! ❤️

We’re thrilled it’s already become your top game! And don’t worry if finger snapping isn’t your thing — as long as you make an effort, it’ll register just fine. This short video of our Creative Director playing one of the hardest levels shows just how forgiving it is:
👉 https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=adWSvKvoC-A

Thanks again for the love — and welcome to the next generation of rhythm gaming!

P.S. — As you’re enjoying BEATABLE, we’d love it if you mentioned it to your friends. We’ve got next to zero marketing budget, so we’re relying on word of mouth to help spread the word! 😊

reviews.review_description: Please get more songs and sound packs to buy. This game could be the next beat saber. I love this. More EDM songs for sure. This game could go far with the EDM and dance songs. I love the groovy songs.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the 5-star review — we love hearing this! 💛

We’re thrilled that you’re enjoying BEATABLE and feeling those groovy songs!
You’re absolutely right — EDM and dance tracks are a perfect fit for the game. We’ve just added 2 new EDM songs, and 10 more tracks are already on the way (free!), with more to come as we grow.

We’re also actively exploring licensed music and sound packs — so stay tuned!

Thanks again for the support — and for believing in the potential of BEATABLE!

P.S. — As you’re enjoying BEATABLE, we’d love it if you mentioned it to your friends. We’ve got next to zero marketing budget, so we’re relying on word of mouth to help spread the word! 😊

reviews.review_description: Great game, played it until my VR died—best game of all time. But my hand hurts after a little while. I think the game would be better with standing and using the Controllers

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the 5-star review — we’re so glad you’re enjoying BEATABLE (and playing until your VR battery gave out is the highest compliment! ❤️)

Totally hear you on hand fatigue — the cool thing is, there are lots of ways players are making the game work for them. Some prefer to stand using a standing desk or even a chest of drawers, while others who want a softer surface use a blanket, pillow, or play right on their bed. We’ve even had players play in front of their drum kit and use that!

Different surfaces can really change the feel and real-world audio feedback of the game — so feel free to experiment and let us know what works best for you!

We want to make BEATABLE as accessible as possible, so we’re also exploring the option of adding controller support for those who prefer it.

Thanks again for the kind words and support — it means a lot to the team!

P.S. — As you’re enjoying BEATABLE, we’d love it if you mentioned it to your friends. We’ve got next to zero marketing budget, so we’re relying on word of mouth to help spread the word! 😊

reviews.review_description: It is a great game, it has a lot of potential to blow up

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the 5-star review! ❤️

We’re glad you’re enjoying BEATABLE — and we agree, there’s huge potential ahead. We’ve just added some new songs, with more free tracks on the way, plus a custom song editor in the works. Big things coming — stay tuned!

P.S. — As you’re enjoying BEATABLE, we’d love it if you mentioned it to your friends. We’ve got next to zero marketing budget, so we’re relying on word of mouth to help spread the word! 😊

reviews.review_description: Having a really good time with this, reminds me of Amplitude and that’s a high compliment!! Can’t wait to see what’s coming.

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much for the 5 stars! Comparing Beatable to Amplitude is seriously high praise — that means a lot to us. We’re really glad you’re having a great time with it. Plenty of exciting updates on the way, so stay tuned — we’re just getting started!

P.S. — as you’re enjoying BEATABLE, we’d love it if you mentioned it to your friends. We’ve got next to zero marketing budget, so we’re relying on word of mouth to help spread the word!

reviews.review_description: Clap hands to start. Oh dear, zero recognition. Uninstall, reinstall, still nothing. So wanted to try this

developer_response.body:
Hi, I’m very sorry to hear about the issues you’re facing. This happened to me too. Please may I ask you to be in hand tracked mode before starting the app? The easiest way to do this is to take the batteries out of your controllers before starting the game. Then start BEATABLE and you won’t have any issues. 

That said, pls do email me at bobby.thandi@xrgames.io if you do still have any problems and I’ll personally trouble shoot it with you.

reviews.review_description: Yes. A promising game indeed. A rhythm game which will be entertaining even for newbies and to show to friends . Previously some hand tracking issues but I guess my lighting was not good enough. Now it works well. Works with half finger gloves too. Happy beatable!

developer_response.body:
Thanks for taking the time to review our game and for buying it. Sorry to hear about you losing your hand tracking most of the time. We've not noticed this in months of play testing. Please can you email me at bobby.thandi@xrgames.io with further information? Which headset? What lighting conditions? Out of direct sunlight? Etc.

I really want to help ensure this doesn't happen to you. We'll hopefully get you sorted so can be posting scores like this! https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=adWSvKvoC-A

P.S. — as you’re enjoying BEATABLE, we’d love it if you mentioned it to your friends. We’ve got next to zero marketing budget, so we’re relying on word of mouth to help spread the word!

reviews.review_description: It's really cool. The hand tracking works really well. Please add more songs.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the kind words! It means a lot to everyone at the studio. And we're working hard to add more songs! We've got 12 tunes we're working on right now which will be rolled out on a frequent basis over the comings weeks and we've got a lot more tunes we're working on securing the rights to.

P.S. — as you’re enjoying BEATABLE, we’d love it if you mentioned it to your friends. We’ve got next to zero marketing budget, so we’re relying on word of mouth to help spread the word!

reviews.review_description: This is awesome !!!!OMG , WOW WOW WOW, Great job 👏Add more song please

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much!! 🙌 We’re buzzing that you’re loving BEATABLE — the energy in your message made our day! And yes, more songs are on the way — 12 additional tracks are already in the works and we're working hard securing the rights to even more well-known tunes. Appreciate the love and support! 💥🎶

P.S. — as you’re enjoying BEATABLE, we’d love it if you mentioned it to your friends. We’ve got next to zero marketing budget, so we’re relying on word of mouth to help spread the word!

reviews.review_description: As a VR newbie this has been the first game that I've been blown away by. The concept is simply amazing and the feels are real. Thank you devs.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the amazing 5-star review — and welcome to VR! ❤️

We’re honoured that BEATABLE was the first game to truly blow you away. Our goal has always been to create something that feels powerful, intuitive, and deeply satisfying — so it means the world to hear that it landed for you.

We’ve got more exciting updates on the way — including a custom song & map editor  — and we can’t wait to show you what’s next.

Thanks again for playing — your support means more than you know.

You’re welcome to join our Discord to hear the latest updates:
👉 https://discord.gg/TcNRzTaMp5

reviews.review_description: Hand tracking at its best. It’s such a simple concept that is well executed! Excited to watch the library of songs continue to grow.

developer_response.body:
Facts! Same here NoeticBridger !

P.S. — If you’re enjoying BEATABLE, we’d love it if you mentioned it to your friends. We’ve got next to zero marketing budget, so we’re relying on word of mouth to help spread the word!

reviews.review_title: Good start, I wish you success
reviews.review_description: Hope to add a beat track like "Spin Rhythm XD" I know you guys will succeed

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the 5-star review and kind words — it really means a lot! 💛

We appreciate the support and love the shoutout to Spin Rhythm XD — such a great reference. We’re just getting started, and feedback like this gives us a big boost. We’ve already added 2 new EDM tracks, with 10 more free songs on the way, and we’re exploring all kinds of features and inspirations for the future.

Thanks again for believing in us — we’re excited to keep building BEATABLE into something truly special!

P.S. — As you’re enjoying BEATABLE, we’d love it if you mentioned it to your friends. We’ve got next to zero marketing budget, so we’re relying on word of mouth to help spread the word! 😊

reviews.review_description:
In short ive been involved in music for 20+ years, playing instruments and mixing music so id like to think games like this are a no brainer for me..

The idea is genius, super simple, very effective (the snap / click to passthrough is so simple but just feels cool)

BUT it really needs extra music FAST, it seems half the set of songs so far is rock themed and that leaves 4 or 5 left. I thought it would launch with 20+ songs with how simple the game is.

1 suggestion also, some kind of audio cue on each hit. Games like synth riders do it and youd be surprised how much better it is to hear a slight sound when getting the hits over nothing, especially when you using hands so your not even getting feedback other then the hit on the table which i thought would be enough but it really isnt.

Ill come back in a few weeks and hopefully the library has doubled with a better variety of music and not all just stuff going into the i noticed in game.

But either way its still very cool, fresh and hopefully has a big future to come.

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much for the honest and detailed feedback — we really appreciate you taking the time to share your thoughts, especially with your background in music. That kind of perspective is invaluable to us.

We’re really glad you’re enjoying the core idea and the simplicity of the passthrough gesture — that’s one of those little touches we hoped would resonate, so it’s great to hear that it landed.

You're absolutely right on the music front. While we wanted to get BEATABLE into players' hands early to start building with the community, we hear you loud and clear — the tracklist needs to grow, and fast. We’ve got 12 additional tracks already in the works, rolling out over the coming weeks, and we’re actively working to secure rights to more songs across a wider variety of genres to avoid things feeling too rock-heavy.

Also, your point about audio feedback is a really smart callout. We’ve been internally discussing how to improve hit feedback — especially since, as you said, you’re not holding anything in your hands. The idea of subtle audio cues per hit is now on our radar in a much bigger way thanks to your note.

I’d love for you to share a top 10 track list you’d want me to try and secure — feel free to send it directly to me at bobby.thandi@xrgames.io and I’ll see what I can do. Always good to have a north star to aim for!

We hope you’ll stick with us and check back in a few weeks — this is just the beginning, and feedback like yours is exactly what’s going to help shape BEATABLE into something even stronger.

Thanks again @BeatzCreations

P.S. — as you’re enjoying BEATABLE, we’d love it if you mentioned it to your friends. We’ve got next to zero marketing budget, so we’re relying on word of mouth to help spread the word!

reviews.review_description: It requires the system update, but my horizon system is up to date ,can anyone help?

developer_response.body:
Sorry to hear about that Tr3v0rr, please can you email me at bobby.thandi@xrgames.io with a screenshot of the message you're seeing and I'll help you with the system update. Then we'll have you playing BEATABLE in no time!

P.S. — if you get it working and you’re enjoying BEATABLE, we’d love it if you mentioned it to your friends. We’ve got next to zero marketing budget, so we’re relying on word of mouth to help spread the word!

reviews.review_description: This game is awesome! Very unique, very addictive and super fun!!! I cant wait for more songs to be added! The only thing i cant do is click my fingers 😅 an option to turn that off would be great lol.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the awesome 5-star review! ❤️

We’re thrilled to hear you’re loving BEATABLE — unique, addictive, and fun is exactly what we were aiming for!

Totally hear you on the finger snapping — the good news is, you don’t need a perfect snap for it to register. As long as you make a clear effort, it’ll work just fine. This quick video from our Creative Director, Chuan, shows that in action — even a basic snapping motion gets picked up:
👉 https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=adWSvKvoC-A

And yes — more songs are on the way, plus a custom song editor launching next month so you can create and share your own tracks. 🎶

Thanks again for your support — and we’re so glad you’re part of the BEATABLE community!

P.S. — If you’re enjoying BEATABLE, we’d love it if you mentioned it to your friends. We’ve got next to zero marketing budget, so word of mouth really helps! 😊

reviews.review_description: This was a gamble without reviews but for $10 you can’t go wrong. Small library out the gate but I’ve enjoyed all the songs. At lot easier on my wrists than other rhythm games and I always play desk drums anyway so this was a seamless buy. I cannot wait to see more songs and updates!

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much for taking a chance on BEATABLE — and for the 5 stars! We’re really glad it hit the mark for you, especially with that desk-drummer background — sounds like a perfect fit. Totally hear you on the song library; we’ve got 12 additional tracks in the works and plenty more to come in future updates. Appreciate the support early on — more fun on the way!

P.S. — as you’re enjoying BEATABLE, we’d love it if you mentioned it to your friends. We’ve got next to zero marketing budget, so we’re relying on word of mouth to help spread the word!

reviews.review_title: A game that shows potential for developm
reviews.review_description:
It's really fun to be able to play along with the rhythm using my own hands.
The only regret is that the effects aren't visible when holding long notes, so the impact isn't felt well, and there aren't many songs that really catch my eye. I think it would be good if you added more genres of songs. Like customization elements.
I look forward to constant updates.

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much for the 5 stars and kind words! Great to hear you're enjoying playing BEATABLE with just your hands — that core interaction is something we’re really proud of. Noted your feedback on the long note effects and music variety. We’ve got plans to improve the visual impact during those moments, and we’re actively working on adding more music across a range of genres. Customisation is definitely on our radar too. Appreciate you being part of the early journey — lots more to come!

P.S. — as you’re enjoying BEATABLE, we’d love it if you mentioned it to your friends. We’ve got next to zero marketing budget, so we’re relying on word of mouth to help spread the word!

reviews.review_description: new way to feel music

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the 5-star review! ❤️

We’re thrilled that BEATABLE gave you a whole new way to feel music — that’s exactly what we set out to create. We’ve got more songs on the way and a custom song editor launching later this month, so there’s a lot more to come.

Thanks again for the support — we’re just getting started!

P.S. — As you’re enjoying BEATABLE, we’d love it if you shared it with your friends. We’ve got next to zero marketing budget, so word of mouth really helps! 😊

reviews.review_title: Amazing change to the Rhythm Genre
reviews.review_description: I play a load of different rhythm games, and I have to say this is such a refreshing and new way to play. I love it. It's extremely responsive and can detect the slightest hand movement. I have been playing the last two days, and I have to say I am addicted. Great job, and I can't wait for more songs to be added.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the 5 stars and the kind words! 🙌 We're buzzing that BEATABLE brought something fresh to your rhythm game lineup — that’s exactly what we set out to do. So glad to hear the responsiveness is hitting the mark and that you’re already hooked after just a couple of days!

We’ve got 12 new songs already in the works, rolling out soon — and plenty more beyond that. Thanks again for the support — more beats incoming! 🎶👏

reviews.review_title: One of the best vr rythm game
reviews.review_description:
I really enjoy japanese rythme games and there is one which is similar. How suprised I was when I saw that it was possible with only a table. The feeling is really nice and the precision is quite good. I love the mechanisme (clapping hands, snapping fingers). 

A nice addition (in plus of more musical content) would be to add hands wave mechanism as well.

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much for the 5 stars — that means a lot to the whole team! We're big fans of Japanese rhythm games too, so it’s amazing to hear that BEATABLE gave you a similar vibe. Really glad the precision and feel landed well for you, and that you’re enjoying the snapping and clapping mechanics — those tactile interactions are a big part of our design philosophy.

We’ve also got something special in the works: we’re currently producing a new track in collaboration with a professional Japanese singer, and we can’t wait to get that track into the game. Plus, we’ve got 12 additional tracks already being prepared for rollout in the coming weeks. Thanks again for your support — more music and magic to come! 🎶👏

P.S. — as you’re enjoying BEATABLE, we’d love it if you mentioned it to your friends. We’ve got next to zero marketing budget, so we’re relying on word of mouth to help spread the word!

reviews.review_description: It doesn't look like much in trailers or gameplay vids, so I was surprised how much fun I started having immediately. It's like Unplugged: Air Guitar meets Ragnarock. The hand tracking works very very well (I did have to remove the controller batteries, as suggested, because the game kept seeing them, a tiny inconvenience). I don't recognize any of the songs (doesn't mean much. I'm still stuck in the 80s and alternative rock) but they are still enjoyable with a good beat, and that's all you need. The price is right in the sweet spot for what's here. I realize it's early access, but if I had a wishlist it would be for more environments and more exciting colors/effects when you hit a streak. Very glad I took a punt on this!

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the amazing 5-star review — we’re really glad you took a chance on BEATABLE and that it paid off! 💛

Great to hear the hand tracking worked well for you (and yes — removing the controller batteries can definitely help with hand detection, even if it’s a small extra step).

While you might not recognise the current tracks, we’re glad you're still enjoying the beats — and we’ve got 12 new songs on the way, including 2 EDM tracks arriving very soon, with even more variety planned for future updates.

We’ve also noted your wishlist: more environments and punchier visual effects on streaks are definitely things we’d love to explore as we continue to grow the game.

Thanks again for giving BEATABLE a shot and for sharing such thoughtful feedback — it really means a lot!

P.S. — As you’re enjoying BEATABLE, we’d love it if you mentioned it to your friends. We’ve got next to zero marketing budget, so we’re relying on word of mouth to help spread the word! 😊

reviews.review_description:
At first I was feeling confused and unsure if I'd made a poor choice in purchasing. First couple of tries on any song kept telling me that  the notes were Perfect/Good AND Missed at the same time. Think the devs might need to look into something here as even if the notes were hit  and scoring they flashed up Missed. Controls can be responsive, sometimes a little too much in the menus so that tracks or menu options skip to the previous/next without meaning to. 
 Aside from that, it got enjoyable, but as these aren't well known songs you're going to need a couple of playthroughs before you start scoring higher and getting a feel for each track. Then it clicks and you get that "one more go" experience. Overall I like it. But it needs more songs from better known artists, and hopefully, like Synth Riders or Beat Saber, an option to add custom tracks in the future.

developer_response.body:
Many thanks for reviewing our game and buying it! Means a lot to us. Totally get what you mean about needing a few runs to really click with the tracks. That “one more go” feeling is exactly what we’re aiming for, so it’s great to hear you found it. And yes, we hear you loud and clear on wanting more songs and more recognisable artists. That’s something we’re exploring, along with options for player-created/custom tracks down the line.

Re this comment: "first couple of tries on any song kept telling me that the notes were Perfect/Good AND Missed at the same time. " -- I've not noticed that myself in months of testing and really want to get to the bottom of this. Any chance you could record a video of this and send it to me to investigate? bobby.thandi@xrgames.io

Thanks again for the feedback — lots more to come!

P.S. — as you’re enjoying BEATABLE, we’d love it if you mentioned it to your friends. We’ve got next to zero marketing budget, so we’re relying on word of mouth to help spread the word!

reviews.review_title: This one has (potentially) a big future.
reviews.review_description: This will be my first review here, because I feel i have to. Usually i dont like the reviewsection, because it feels like many reviews are bought.  Well, let me tell you,  mine is not! I love rytmgames, and this one has potential to be the next big thing. It works absolutely amazing, and what we get is really well done. As you already aware of; music and rytmgame is depending on the music. Needs more music asap.  And it would add so much to have some kind of respons when we hit the notes, now that we dont have rumble. If this one gets modsupport and custom song; its gonna be huge. Besides songpacks you might release cosmetic content like glows etc. I think if you handle this right you will succeed. The day we get custom songs; i probably change this to a 5 star 🫡

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much for taking the time to write your first review — and for the 4 stars! It means a lot that you'd break your usual silence for BEATABLE, especially as a rhythm game fan. We're glad you can see the potential — we’re building this with the same belief.

You’re absolutely right: rhythm games live and die by their music. We’ve got 12 additional tracks and maps already in production, rolling out over the coming weeks, and we’re actively working on licensing more well-known tracks too. Your point about feedback is also well taken — we’re currently exploring ways to add more satisfying hit response, even without haptics.

As for mod support and custom songs — we hear you loud and clear. It’s definitely something we’re planning right now as we know it could take BEATABLE to the next level.

Appreciate the thoughtful feedback @TheMassMonster — we’ll do everything we can to earn that 5-star update 🫡

P.S. — as you’re enjoying BEATABLE, we’d love it if you mentioned it to your friends. We’ve got next to zero marketing budget, so we’re relying on word of mouth to help spread the word!

reviews.review_description: Fun and adictive. Great use of hand tracking. Tracks are fun to play and Devs have promised even more! I would be willing to pay for some more licensed music, would be cool to custom beat map. Awesome price too, grab it, you won't regret.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the awesome 5-star review! 💛

We’re thrilled to hear you’re finding BEATABLE fun and addictive — and that the hand tracking is working well for you!
Great to know you’re enjoying the current tracks too. As promised, 2 new EDM tracks just dropped, and 10 more free songs are already on the way, with even more planned after that.

We hear you on licensed music and custom beat mapping — both are features we’d love to explore as the game grows.

Thanks again for the kind words and support — and yes, we agree: now’s the perfect time to grab it 😉

P.S. — As you’re enjoying BEATABLE, we’d love it if you mentioned it to your friends. We’ve got next to zero marketing budget, so we’re relying on word of mouth to help spread the word! 😊

reviews.review_title: Love it but needs more songs
reviews.review_description: Great game. Simply needs more songs and then its one of the most original and best rhythm games out there.

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much for the kind words and support! We're thrilled you're enjoying the game. 🙌 You're absolutely right — more songs will take it to the next level. We've got 12 new tunes/maps currently in development, which we'll be drip-releasing over the coming weeks. We're also working on deals with music licensing labels to bring even more variety to the game. Stay tuned — exciting things are on the way!

P.S. — as you’re enjoying BEATABLE, we’d love it if you mentioned it to your friends. We’ve got next to zero marketing budget, so we’re relying on word of mouth to help spread the word!

reviews.review_description: This game is fantastic!!  I can't wait until they add more songs!

developer_response.body:
We’re so glad you’re loving BEATABLE! More songs are on the way—we’re currently working on 12 new tracks, and they’ll be added for free in future updates.

P.S. — If you’re enjoying BEATABLE, we’d love it if you told your friends. We’ve got next to zero marketing budget, so word of mouth really helps us out!

reviews.review_description: Amazing work, very fun gameplay, level editor will be fire!

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for the 5 stars! 🙌 We're thrilled you're loving the gameplay — and yes, we agree... when that level editor drops, it's gonna be fire. 🔥 Appreciate the support — more exciting stuff coming soon!
